Off Calendar Sep-09-2024 Continued To Apr-02-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Sep-04-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant DM 2465 Van Ness Holdings Member LLC's (DM) motion to compel arbitration is granted. The proceedings are stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration.

DM commenced an arbitration against Plaintiff Navid Sarrafzadeh (Sarrafzadeh) for declaratory judgment. The arbitrator issued a final award, which this Court confirmed. The Court later entered judgment on the award to DM for reasonable attorneys' fees and costs incurred in the arbitration. Sarrafzadeh filed a notice of appeal of this judgment, which remains pending. Sarrafzadeh then filed the complaint in the instant action. DM now files this motion to compel arbitration.

It is the burden of the party seeking to compel arbitration to establish the existence of an enforceable arbitration agreement. (Diaz v. Sohnen Enterprises (2019) 34 Cal.App.5th 126, 129.) "A movant can bear this initial burden 'by attaching a copy of the arbitration agreement purportedly bearing the opposing party's signature.'" (Iyere v. Wise Auto Group (2023) 87 Cal.App.5th 747, 755, citing Espejo v. Southern California Permanente Medical Group (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 1047, 1060.) DM meets its burden by pointing to a copy of the signed arbitration agreement that Sarrafzadeh attached to his complaint. (Complaint, Exhibit A, 12.18.)

The burden shifts to Sarrafzadeh to either dispute the agreement's existence or provide a defense to enforcement, in this case, unconscionability. The arbitration agreement is not substantively unconscionable. Sarrafzadeh has not met his burden to show that JAMS is not a neutral arbitrator. The JAMS rules do not impermissibly limit remedies or discovery, and a written award is provided for. The case is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration under CCP 1281.4.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
